description: Fix 9 weak stress test assertions that always pass regardless of system behavior. Replace tautological checks (typeof isError === 'boolean', settlement-only status checks) with concrete correctness specifications that will FAIL if the system misbehaves. These tests should define correct behavior as a specification — if the test fails, the system has a bug to fix.

userStories.title: Fix hot-reload in-flight assertion in stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Replace the tautological `typeof result.isError === 'boolean'` check with a meaningful assertion: in-flight calls must either succeed (isError false with valid content) or fail with a specific dispatch/disconnect error message — not silently return garbage.
acceptanceCriteria: The hot-reload test no longer uses `typeof result.isError === 'boolean'`

Each fulfilled in-flight result is validated: if isError is false, the result content must be non-empty valid JSON; if isError is true, the error text must contain one of: 'disconnected', 'timed out', 'Dispatch', 'dispatch', 'worker'
Rejected promises (MCP client transport errors) are counted separately and their error message is logged for debugging
After all in-flight calls settle, the test still verifies recovery (fresh echo call succeeds with exact message match)
The test adds a timing assertion: all in-flight calls must settle within 15 seconds of the hot reload trigger (not the full 30s dispatch timeout)

userStories.title: Fix permission-change in-flight assertion in stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Replace the tautological `typeof slowResult.isError === 'boolean'` with a hard assertion that the in-flight call SUCCEEDS (not just 'is a boolean'). Permission is checked once at dispatch entry — an in-flight call that already passed the check MUST complete successfully.
acceptanceCriteria: The permission-change test no longer uses `typeof slowResult.isError === 'boolean'`
The in-flight slow call is asserted to succeed: `expect(slowResult.isError).not.toBe(true)` — because the permission check already passed before dispatch
If the system incorrectly kills in-flight calls on permission change, this test FAILS (that's the point — it would be a bug to fix)
userStories.notes:
Files:
- e2e/stress-mcp-dispatch.e2e.ts (modify permission-change test)

Context:
The MCP server checks permission ONCE at dispatch entry (`mcp-tool-dispatch.ts`). After the check passes, the call is dispatched to the extension. Changing permission afterward does NOT retroactively kill in-flight calls. So the in-flight call MUST succeed.

Current weak code at line 289:
```typescript
expect(typeof slowResult.isError === 'boolean').toBe(true);
```

Replace with:
```typescript
// The in-flight call already passed the permission check before dispatch.
// It MUST complete successfully — permission changes only affect NEW calls.
expect(slowResult.isError).not.toBe(true);
```

Constraints:
- Do NOT change the subsequent rejection test (it's already correct — checks exact error message)
- Do NOT change the recovery test
- If this assertion fails in CI, it means the system has a real bug: in-flight calls are being killed by permission changes, which is incorrect behavior

userStories.title: Fix mid-navigation assertion in stress-adapter-injection.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Replace `expect(typeof midNavResult.isError).toBe('boolean')` with a concrete assertion: the tool call must resolve within 10 seconds (not hang), and if it errors, the error must identify the cause.
acceptanceCriteria: The navigation test no longer uses `typeof midNavResult.isError`
The tool call is wrapped in a Promise.race with a 10-second timeout — if it hangs, the test fails with 'tool call hung for 10s after tab navigation'
If the call succeeds (isError false), the content is verified as non-empty
If the call errors (isError true), the error text must contain 'tab', 'closed', 'navigat', or 'disconnect' (identifying the cause)
The post-navigation recovery assertion (echo after navigating back) remains unchanged

userStories.title: Fix server-restart in-flight assertion in stress-network-auth.e2e.ts
userStories.description: Replace the settlement-only check with validation that in-flight calls fail with identifiable disconnect errors, and add a timing bound.
acceptanceCriteria: The server restart test no longer uses `['fulfilled', 'rejected'].toContain(result.status)` as its only in-flight check
Each fulfilled result is checked: if isError is true, error text must contain 'disconnected', 'timed out', or 'dispatch'
Each rejected result is counted and its error message logged
A timing assertion is added: all in-flight calls must settle within 15 seconds of server kill
The post-restart recovery verification (fresh echo on new server) remains unchanged

userStories.title: Fix cross-system permission toggle in-flight assertion
userStories.description: Replace `expect(slowResult.status).toBe('fulfilled')` with a concrete check: the slow call on a DIFFERENT tool than the toggled one MUST succeed.
acceptanceCriteria: The permission toggle test in stress-cross-system.e2e.ts no longer checks only `.status === 'fulfilled'`
The slow call result is unwrapped and its isError is asserted false — because permission was toggled on a DIFFERENT tool (echo), not the running tool (slow_with_progress)
If the system incorrectly cancels unrelated in-flight calls during permission changes, this test FAILS

userStories.title: Fix cross-system config-change in-flight assertion and require at least one failure
userStories.description: Replace settlement-only check with validation that at least one in-flight call FAILED due to plugin removal (proving the removal took effect), and errors identify the cause.
acceptanceCriteria: The config-change test no longer uses `['fulfilled', 'rejected'].toContain(result.status)` as its only check
At least one of the 3 in-flight calls must have failed (isError true or rejected) — proving the plugin removal took effect during dispatch
Failed calls must have errors containing 'not found', 'unavailable', 'removed', 'disconnected', or 'no matching tab'
If ALL 3 calls succeed despite the plugin being removed, the test FAILS (the removal didn't take effect, which is a bug)

userStories.title: Fix full-system-stress error swallowing and strengthen recovery assertion
userStories.description: Remove blanket .catch(() => {}) from client loops, replace 'at least 1 client recovers' with 'ALL 3 clients recover', and track error counts during chaos for debugging.
acceptanceCriteria: Client loops no longer use `.catch(() => {})` — errors during config removal are caught but COUNTED and their messages RECORDED
After chaos, ALL 3 MCP clients must make a successful echo call (not just 'at least 1')
The test reports the number of failed calls during chaos (for debugging) but does not assert a specific count (failures during config removal are expected)
Side panel assertions remain unchanged (zero pageerror, plugin visible)
